title: Tender Tunisian Turkey Neck Tagine
description: A rich and indulgent Tunisian dish featuring tender turkey neck, this tagine is a low-carb friendly option for a special occasion. The combination of spices and slow cooking creates a fatty and pillowy texture.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper and cook for 1 minute.
Add the turkey neck and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the chicken broth, dried apricots, and preserved lemon.
Cover the tagine or Dutch oven and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours.
Remove the tagine from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Serve the turkey neck and sauce over cauliflower rice or zucchini noodles.
